name: [smoke] renders all 5 canonical sizes without crashing
[smoke] renders with start + end slots simultaneously
[smoke] size="m" with Icon start + end slots
[smoke] icon-only badge (no children, start slot only)
[fn] high attention produces a non-transparent background
[fn] medium attention produces a non-transparent background
[fn] low attention produces a transparent background (ghost)
[fn] high and medium attention produce visually distinct backgrounds
[fn] default (no attention, no variant) renders with bold variant — filled background
[fn] explicit variant="ghost" overrides attention="high" — variant wins completely
[fn] explicit variant="bold" overrides attention="low" — variant wins
[fn] bold and subtle variants produce different non-transparent backgrounds
[fn] ghost variant has a hairline border (borderWidth > 0)
[fn] ghost variant has a non-null borderColor
[fn] bold variant has no border (borderWidth absent or 0)
[fn] default appearance resolves to "sparkle" (no appearance prop given)
[fn] appearance="primary" produces different paint than appearance="neutral"
[fn] semantic appearances (negative, positive) render without crashing
[fn] appearance="neutral" produces a non-transparent background (bold variant)
[fn] appearance="auto" resolves identically to no appearance (sparkle)
[fn] each size has the correct height from spacing tokens
[fn] heights are strictly increasing from xs to xl
[fn] minWidth equals height for each size (square baseline before text)
[fn] XL padding should be spacing["2"] — same as L, not spacing["1-5"]
[fn] start slot ReactNode is rendered in the tree
[fn] end slot ReactNode is rendered in the tree
[fn] start AND end slots both appear when provided simultaneously
[fn] string start prop is silently discarded — slot container not rendered
[fn] null start prop → no slot rendered
[fn] false start prop → no slot rendered
[fn] testID prop is forwarded to root View
[a11y] numeric children become a string accessibilityLabel
[a11y] aria-label sets accessibilityLabel regardless of children
[a11y] aria-label overrides string children for accessibilityLabel
[a11y] icon-only badge with ReactElement children + no aria-label → accessible=false
[a11y] ReactElement children → accessibilityRole should be "none", not "text"
[a11y] no children, no aria-label → accessible=false, role is not set
[a11y] live region is polite even for icon-only badge
[a11y] slot content is a11y-hidden when Badge has a label — nested aria-labels disappear
[a11y] slot content IS accessible when Badge has no label
